requirements_scoping.reasoning: Clear functional and non-functional requirements with quantified non-functional targets (p99 latency ≤50ms, 99.99% uptime, durability ≥99.999%). Scale assumptions stated (100k QPS peak, 200M URLs). No explicit non-goals section, so not a 5.

capacity_estimation.reasoning: Full estimation chain: assumptions to QPS, storage, bandwidth, component sizing. Numbers demonstrably drive design decisions (shard counts, Redis nodes). Includes sanity check (after roll-up, aggregates <10GB).

architecture_coherence.reasoning: Every component serves a stated requirement, connections are sensible, data flow diagram provided. Architecture description is informative, with justifications (e.g., two-tier caching). No hallucinations or dead weight.

deep_dive_depth.reasoning: Multiple deep-dives: ID generation (with tradeoff comparison), sharding strategy (concrete hash-modulo, range allocation), caching layers (three tiers with TTLs and hit-rate targets), and failure handling for hot keys/cache miss cascade. Non-obvious edge case (hot key) addressed with virtual nodes and dedicated hot-key cache tier.

tradeoffs_failure_modes.reasoning: Extensive tradeoffs table with alternatives and when to switch. Failure modes table covers many scenarios including non-obvious ones (cache purge failure, network partition, ID generator crash with mitigation). Specific mitigations provided. Design acknowledges limits implicitly through tradeoffs.
